text:
These companies are locking in the physical substrate behind the next decade of AI compute and that substrate is power.

You can order GPUs from $NVDA or $AMD or TPUs from $GOOGL but you can’t just order gigawatts so now the question becomes who can actually deliver it. 

Contracted power: 
• $IREN ~4.5GW
• $CRWV ~3.1GW
• $NBIS ~2.0GW
• $WULF ~643MW 
• $CIFR ~600MW
• $APLD ~600MW
• $CORZ ~590MW
• $HUT ~245MW

Absolutely wild that $COST makes ~$16M a day before anyone walks through the doors.

No wonder the $1.50 hot dog is safe.

Tehran’s Shahran oil depot engulfed in flames after overnight Israeli airstrikes with thick black smoke blanketing the city creating whats described as an “apocalyptic morning.”

Officials warn massive fires could trigger toxic acid rain with residents advised to stay indoors.

$NVDA latest Rubin AI chip requires ~300GB of memory (up from 80GB on the H100) showing how each generation of AI hardware demands far more DRAM.

At hyperscaler scale, this is pulling massive amounts of DRAM out of market turning memory into a key bottleneck in the AI buildout.

$HIMS CEO Andrew Dudum says the GLP-1 obesity drug market could become one of the largest categories in healthcare after $NVO ended its dispute with Hims & agreed to partner.

He expects competition to push prices from ~$1,500/month toward ~$50 as supply grows & patents expire.
$HIMS JUMPS ~35% AFTER ANNOUNCING A NEW PARTNERSHIP WITH $NVO

Link below to catch the full episode:

piped.video/njm8IdzoYY0?si=pfXQ…

FUTURUM EQUITIES PODCAST EPISODE #33

Daniel and I covered five big themes:

• Stagflation just landed: breaking down the -92K jobs print and why the Fed is now stuck
• Earnings deep dive: $AVGO ($100B AI revenue target by 2027 + supply chain locked through 2028) & $MRVL (finally getting respect + why optical networking is the next leg)
• $META still capacity constrained: what that means for AI infra names like $NBIS, $IREN, $CIFR and $CRWV
• Anthropic vs OpenAI: who really owns the enterprise and is $MSFT at risk?
• The burn-in testing trade no one is talking about: $AEHR

Plus a 10-minute live Q&A at the end. 

Watch the full episode at the link below ⤵️

10 COMPANIES WITH THE STRONGEST EARNINGS CYCLE

1. $PLTR | Palantir
2. $AAOI | Applied Optoelectronics
3. $TSM | TSMC
4. $SNDK | Sandisk 
5. $LITE | Lumentum
6. $META | Meta
7. $GOOGL | Google
8. $FSLY | Fastly
9. $AXON | Axon
10. $SOFI | SoFi

Chamath says AI infrastructure is driving an off-grid energy revolution as hyperscalers bypass grid bottlenecks with behind-the-meter power.

He says co-location with dedicated power sources is emerging as the solution when the grid can’t meet AI demand.
Typical gutless BS

I met Rev. Jackson. I ate with him, enjoyed the company of two of his sons & cherish  my conversations with him. Watched him calm a crying baby in a restaurant just by holding her. His life should be celebrated not politicized.
Before he retires, LeFlop will degrade his legacy enough to fall out of the top 10. GOAT? He couldn't hold MJs jock @KingJames

Iran has attacked merchant ships in the Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman. 

The US expanded the war zone to the Indian Ocean by sinking the frigate INS Dena. 

The US does not understand shipping, but the weak point is right in front of them: Iranian tankers.

If this is true...The Mucker's @KeithMcCullough  signal remains undefeated, it' had every subsc. pulling profits on ea. spike the past week. re-allocating and ready to reload on the next dip. No narrative needed...and in fact a front runner of the narrative.

Always love Hedgeye on the timeline during high vol. Hundreds of narratives barking about where the puck is headed and Hedgeye is just like, well we buy/own stuff that’s breaking out and sell what’s breaking down.

AI workloads could account for 50% of all data cetner capacity by 2030, up from ~25% in 2025, per JLL, with AI inference workloads overtaking training as the dominant driver in 2027.

A lot of oil in the USA!  This disruption really hurts China and then some European pain.   While the US has to pay a higher price at the pump, having happened on the heels of capturing Venezuelan oil; one could ask if closing the the Strait was a strategic move the US wanted to see as a Chinese squeeze?

Oil priced in yen has super-spiked.

Japan imports 94% of its crude from the Middle East.

The BOJ is trapped. Yen weakening. Import costs exploding.

The reverse carry trade isn't a theory anymore.

It was always about oil.

Russell 2000 down over 4% in a week.

Small caps need cheap credit, domestic demand, and stable energy costs.

They have none of those.

IWM has underperformed SPY by 600+ basis points over the past month.

When the most economically sensitive part of the market leads lower, believe it.

February payrolls: -92,000.

Wall Street expected +50,000.

3-month average: +6,000 jobs per month. The economy needs 100,000+ just to tread water.

December revised to -17,000. Third negative print in five months.

The labor market isn't softening. It's cracking.

S&P 500 YTD return: -1.5%.

After two consecutive years of 20%+ gains.

The market gave back the entire January rally in a week.

Most people still positioned for 2024-2025 to repeat. It won't.

Regime changes don't announce themselves. They just show up in the data.

A few weeks back I released a version of a song I wrote many years ago called “Hold On” as I’ve been trying to find my way back to Christ.

I just created a version with female vocals and a harder hook and I have goosebumps.

What do you think?

VIX surged nearly 50% in a week.

Sitting near 30. SPX skew in the 99th percentile.

This isn't hedging. This is the market pricing in something structural breaking.

The last three times VIX hit 30 with skew this elevated: late 2018, March 2020, and September 2022.

All required a Fed response. The Fed can't respond now.

The market gave back the entire January rally in a week.

Most people still positioned for 2024-2025 to repeat. It won't.

Regime changes don't announce themselves. They just show up in the data.

Oil up 36% in a single week.

WTI at $91. Brent above $92.

Qatar's energy minister warning Gulf exporters could shut down production within weeks.

The last time oil moved this fast was the Russia-Ukraine invasion.

The difference? The SPR was full then. It isn't now.
